fact: June 29 is exactly 180 days from the end of the year - it's like the calendar's halfway point!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when fireflies are at their flashiest, blinking like tiny disco balls in your backyard!
The sun sets super late on June 29 in places like Alaska - some spots get sunlight until almost midnight!
Cherry trees in many places are dropping their fruit right now, creating slippery, sticky sidewalks that look like nature's candy explosion!
June 29 falls smack in the middle of Cancer season, when the moon's influence is supposed to be extra strong on emotions and tides!
Cicadas are screaming their loudest around this time in hot places - they can be louder than a motorcycle!
This date is only 8 days after the summer solstice, so you're still getting some of the longest days of the entire year!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, June 29 is deep winter - penguins are huddling for warmth while you might be swimming!
Mosquitoes are at peak annoyance levels around this date in many places because the weather is perfectly warm and humid for their gross breeding!
Strawberry plants are finishing up their fruiting season right around now, which is why your fingers might be stained red from berry picking!
Ocean water temperatures hit their stride around this time - not too cold from spring, but not scorching hot from peak summer yet!
